revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] {{Infobox deity|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|navigation|WD=Q102066}}; {{ill|saliculture|WD=Q62083214}}}} '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki mythology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本神話|1=Japanese mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q814158}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|en|Greek mythology|WD=Q34726|lt=Greek mythology}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|en|Proteus|WD=Q191481|lt=Proteus}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shiogama|en|Shiogama|WD=Q496448|lt=Shiogama}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}’s account of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}.
During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山幸彦と海幸彦}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}

Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shiogama|en|Shiogama|WD=Q496448|lt=Shiogama}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}’s account of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}.
During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山幸彦と海幸彦}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] {{Infobox deity|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|navigation|WD=Q102066}}; {{ill|saliculture|WD=Q62083214}}}} '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki mythology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本神話|1=Japanese mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q814158}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|en|Greek mythology|WD=Q34726|lt=Greek mythology}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|en|Proteus|WD=Q191481|lt=Proteus}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shiogama|en|Shiogama|WD=Q496448|lt=Shiogama}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}’s account of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}.
During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] {{Infobox deity|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|navigation|WD=Q102066|1=Navigation}}; {{ill|saliculture|WD=Q62083214|1=saliculture}}}} '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki mythology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本神話|1=Japanese mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q814158}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|en|Greek mythology|WD=Q34726|lt=Greek mythology|1=Greek mythology}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|en|Proteus|WD=Q191481|lt=Proteus|1=Proteus}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in {{ill|Shiogama|en|Shiogama|WD=Q496448|lt=Shiogama|1=Shiogama}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}’s account of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|1=Tenson kōrin}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori|1=Hoori}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}.
During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|1=Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)

== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|1=Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山幸彦と海幸彦}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiotsuchi]][[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:シオツチオジ]][[zh:鹽土老翁]] {{Infobox deity|deity_of=Kami of {{ill|navigation|WD=Q102066|1=Navigation}}; {{ill|saliculture|WD=Q62083214|1=saliculture}}}} '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki mythology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本神話|1=Japanese mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q814158}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|en|Greek mythology|WD=Q34726|lt=Greek mythology|1=Greek mythology}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|en|Proteus|WD=Q191481|lt=Proteus|1=Proteus}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in {{ill|Shiogama|en|Shiogama|WD=Q496448|lt=Shiogama|1=Shiogama}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}’s account of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|1=Tenson kōrin}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori|1=Hoori}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}.
During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|qq=}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamu Shrine|1=Takamuro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669159}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|en|Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki|WD=Q3439900|lt=Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|1=Rokusho Shrine, Okazaki}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|qq=}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|塩屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)|qq=}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山幸彦と海幸彦}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
